title: Module 1
content:
Circuit theorems: DC and AC circuit analysis with dependent and
independent sources applying Superposition principle, Source transformation,
Thevenin’s, Norton’s and Maximum Power Transfer theorems - Reciprocity
theorem.
Two port networks: Driving point and transfer functions – Z, Y, h and T
parameters - Conditions for symmetry & reciprocity

title: Module 2
content:
Resonance: Introduction – series resonance – parallel resonance –
Definition: Q Factor- Half power frequency resonant frequency –
Bandwidth.
Transformed circuits in s-domain: Transform impedance/admittance of R,
L and C - Mesh analysis and node analysis of transformed circuits in s-
domain. Transfer Function representation – Poles and zeros.

title: Module 3
content:
Transformers: Operating principle, classification, construction, emf
equation, phasor diagrams, equivalent circuit model, losses & efficiency.
D.C. Machines: Operating principle, generator & motor action, construction,
types of excitations, emf & torque equations, power stages & efficiency,
characteristics & application of d.c generators and d.c motors

title: Module 4
content:
Induction Machines: Three-phase induction motors. Principle of operation,
construction, types, emf equation of an AC Machine, torque developed in an
induction motor, equivalent circuit model, torque-speed characteristics,
Single phase induction motors, Types of Single-Phase Induction motor,
starting, application
Synchronous Machines: Construction, types & operating principle of
synchronous generator, voltage regulation-EMF method, Power Angle
characteristics, Synchronous Motor, principle, starting, hunting, damper
windings
